perturbation.original_text: Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement, unless Affiliate violates section 2. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'unless Affiliate violates section 2' introduces an ambiguity regarding commission payments after termination. While the original text states Affiliate will receive commission payments for Approved Accounts during the term, the addition implies that violating section 2 could retroactively void previously earned commissions even after termination. This is contradictory because it's not explicitly clear if previously earned commissions are forfeited or just future ones. Section 5 states that affiliates will continue to receive commission payments for accounts during the term, the perturbation makes it contradict if this is still true if an affiliate violates section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement will commence on the date that the Affiliate Registration Form is approved by Chase and will end when terminated by either party. Either Affiliate or Chase may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, by giving the other party written or e-mail notice of termination. At the time of termination, any links to Chase's Website must be removed immediately. Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement, unless Affiliate violates section 2. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition ('unless Affiliate violates section 2') to the existing clause regarding commission payments. This directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, which guaranteed commission payments for approved accounts during the term of the agreement. The modification introduces ambiguity about whether a violation of section 2 retroactively voids previously earned commissions, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the affiliate's entitlement to those commissions, aligning with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by posting a new agreement on its Website. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the amount of Commissions, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change without notice other than posting such information on the Chase Website. IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING CHASE'S POSTING OF A NEW AGREEMENT ON ITS WEB SITE WILL CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE.
perturbation.changed_text: Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by posting a new agreement on its Website. Notwithstanding this, commission rates are fixed for the first six months of the agreement. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change without notice other than posting such information on the Chase Website. IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING CHASE'S POSTING OF A NEW AGREEMENT ON ITS WEB SITE WILL CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'Notwithstanding this, commission rates are fixed for the first six months of the agreement' contradicts the following sentence 'the amount of Commissions...are subject to change without notice'. This creates an ambiguity: can commission rates be changed within the first six months? The first sentence states that Chase can modify any and all of the terms, however, a phrase after, make it contradict because commision rates are not subject to change during the first six months.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Modification Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by posting a new agreement on its Website. Notwithstanding this, commission rates are fixed for the first six months of the agreement. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change without notice other than posting such information on the Chase Website. IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING CHASE'S POSTING OF A NEW AGREEMENT ON ITS WEB SITE WILL CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text allows Chase to change commission rates at any time. The changed text states commission rates are fixed for the first six months, creating an ambiguity and direct conflict within the same clause. This violates the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because the terms relating to the commission are defined inconsistently within the same part of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Chase will pay Affiliate a fee for each approved credit card account that originates from a link in Affiliate's Website.
perturbation.changed_text: Chase will pay Affiliate a fee for each approved credit card account that originates from a compliant link in Affiliate's Website, provided the affiliate has not been found in violation of section 1.
perturbation.explanation: The added 'provided the affiliate has not been found in violation of section 1' creates ambiguity on the condition for commission payments. The initial statement promises payment for each approved account, but the additional clause introduces a condition that could retroactively deny commission based on violations of section 1 (Restricted Content). This creates a contradiction as it's not clear if compliance with Section 1 is required for only future commissions or retroactively for previously 'approved credit card account'. This contradicts section 3, as it does not outline any conditions on commission payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Referral Fee For each Approved Account (as defined in section 4 below) received through Affiliate's site, Affiliate will earn a referral fee calculated in accordance with the schedule set forth below ("Commission") paid monthly. • Tiered or flat commission based on private offer terms in the network. Chase reserves the right to alter above referral fees from time to time upon written notice to Affiliate of such change.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition ('provided the affiliate has not been found in violation of section 1') for receiving commission that is not present in the contradicted text (Referral Fee For each Approved Account...Affiliate will earn a referral fee calculated in accordance with the schedule set forth below...paid monthly). This creates an ambiguity in the conditions for payment and contradicts the original section, because the original section does not outline any condition on commission payment, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
